lenses.guidance: vv 1-6's Zelophehad's-daughters narrative and vv 14-18's Joseph-tribe complaint pair structurally — both raise inheritance questions, both are answered by procedure. The form makes the partition adjudicative; rights are granted, not assumed.

vv 3-6's Zelophehad's-daughters inheritance enacts Num 27:1-11's earlier ruling. The canonical thread keeps procedural-justice working across the wilderness-to-conquest transition; precedent set in Numbers is honored in Joshua.
